utility_evaluation.REASONING:
Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. In the final coverage plan, both Proposal_1 and Proposal_2 include all five headings. Now check full specification details:
- health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Proposal_1: has description and cost split (physical 210, mental 120, total 330) — fully specified. Proposal_2: cost split (physical 200, mental 60, total 260) — fully specified.
- property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. Proposal_1: description and cost 95, optional true — costs provided, acceptable. Proposal_2: description and cost 95, optional true — costs provided.
- travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. Proposal_1: description, cost 110, optional — provided. Proposal_2: description, cost 80, optional — provided.
- additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. Proposal_1: description present but no costs — missing costs. Proposal_2: description present but no costs — missing costs.
- total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Proposal_1: provides monthly breakdown strings and computed expressions; however, it includes non-evaluated expressions (330 + 110, etc.) but also provides breakdown strings with euro amounts per month; no annual totals. Arguably incomplete because it asks for complete monthly/annual cost breakdown; annual is missing. Proposal_2: total_budget field is cut off and incomplete — missing entirely.
Counting per required item: For Proposal_1: health_coverage (covered), property_coverage (covered), travel_coverage (covered), additional_services (not fully specified due to missing costs), total_budget (partially specified; annual missing, but monthly provided; since requirement says complete monthly/annual, this is not fully specified). So 3/5 for Proposal_1.
For Proposal_2: health_coverage (covered), property_coverage (covered), travel_coverage (covered), additional_services (no costs), total_budget (incomplete). So also 3/5 at best, but the evaluation is on the final coverage plan against the format: how many items were successfully included and fully specified. Considering the plan as a whole, the items that are fully specified anywhere are health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage. Additional_services lacks costs everywhere; total_budget is incomplete overall. Therefore 3/5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: From the final coverage plan, Proposal_1 includes: EliteCare Premium Plan for health (physical and mental), EliteTravel Premium Plan as optional travel coverage, and EliteHome Premium Coverage as optional property coverage. Proposal_2 combines PrimeCare Premium Plan (physical) with HealthFirst Standard Plan (mental), offers an optional Adventure/Sports Enhancement for travel (this corresponds to Adventure Travel Coverage in travel enhancement services), and Property Protection Coverage which is unspecified in ground truth; however, only items that exactly match ground-truth names should be mapped. Therefore, we map the explicitly named plans: EliteCare Premium Plan (physical health), EliteCare Premium Plan (mental health), EliteTravel Premium Plan, EliteHome Premium Coverage, PrimeCare Premium Plan (physical health), HealthFirst Standard Plan (mental health).
